title: Funding Sources — Overview
content:
The Morgan State University Office of Research Administration (ORA) maintains a curated catalog of external funding sources for faculty, principal investigators (PIs), graduate students, and research staff. The page is organized into three layers: (1) general-purpose external funding databases that aggregate opportunities across thousands of sponsors, (2) direct links to federal agency funding portals organized by topical category, and (3) state-level (Maryland) plus private/foundation funding programs.

FIVE EXTERNAL FUNDING DATABASES MSU SUBSCRIBES TO OR RECOMMENDS:
1. Pivot RP (ProQuest) — https://pivot.proquest.com/ — MSU-subscribed comprehensive global funding database. Features saved searches, collaborator discovery, tailored recommendations, alerts, and authoritative funding news/analysis.
2. InfoEd Global SPIN — https://spin.infoedglobal.com/ — World's largest database of sponsored funding opportunities, tracking 40,000+ opportunities across governments, foundations, and commercial entities. Includes SMARTS automated daily/weekly alert tool.
3. Grants.gov — https://www.grants.gov/ — Federal government clearinghouse for all U.S. federal grant opportunities.
4. Grant Forward — https://www.grantforward.com/ — Additional searchable funding opportunity database.
5. SAM.gov Contract Opportunities — https://sam.gov/content/opportunities — Federal contract opportunity portal (formerly FedBizOpps).

FEDERAL SPONSOR CATEGORIES COVERED ON THE PAGE:
- Defense agencies (AFOSR, DARPA, ARO, DoD, VA, DOD SBIR/STTR, ONR)
- Non-Defense agencies (EDA, NASA, NSF, NTIA, USAID, USDA, Commerce, DOE, HHS, HUD, DOJ, State, DOT)
- Health (NIH, SAMHSA)
- Education (Department of Education, Education Daily)
- Arts & Humanities (IMLS, NEA, NEH)
- Environmental Science (NOAA, NREL, EPA)

HBCU/MSI-SPECIFIC FUNDING: The page features a dedicated section listing HBCU/Minority Serving Institution programs from DoD, Department of Education, DOE, DHS, DoT, NASA, NEH, NIH, NPS, and NSF — including high-priority programs for Morgan such as NSF HBCU-UP, NSF EiR, NSF CREST, NIH NIGMS SuRE, NIH NIMHD RCMI, NEH Awards for Faculty at HBCUs, and NASA MUREP.

STATE & PRIVATE FUNDING:
- Maryland Innovation Initiative (MII) — https://www.tedcomd.com/funding/maryland-innovation-initiative
- Maryland E-Nnovation Initiative Fund (MEIF) — https://commerce.maryland.gov/fund/maryland-e-nnovation-initiative-fund-(meif)
- Foundation Center — http://foundationcenter.org/ (private/foundation funding directory).

For questions about access credentials or how to use these resources, contact ORA at ask.ora@morgan.edu or 443-885-4044 (Tyler Hall, Student Service Bldg, Suite 304, Baltimore, MD 21251).
